items.title: AI evolution decoded: Ace investor Vijay Kedia explains it with a simple house-building analogy
items.summary: Veteran investor Vijay Kedia explains AI's evolution using a house-building analogy, from expert advice (LLMs) to autonomous action (agents), collaborative teams (multi-agent), human-level mastery (AGI), and beyond (superintelligence). This framework mirrors his investment philosophy: learn, earn, evolve. However, Kedia's portfolio faced a challenging FY26, with most holdings declining amid market pressures and geopolitical concerns.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $67K as crypto markets stay muted; volatility seen rising ahead
items.summary: Bitcoin hovered near the $67,000 mark on Saturday as crypto markets stayed muted, with traders awaiting a potential volatility spike. While BTC and ETH moved sideways, analysts flagged tightening ranges and bottoming implied volatility. Key levels remain $65K on the downside and $69K on the upside, even as institutional inflows and sentiment show early improvement.

items.title: NCAA Gymnastics ‘Sweet Sixteen’ To Battle This Weekend In Regional Finals
items.summary: The NCAA Gymnastics 'Sweet Sixteen' is set. After 12 weeks of regular-season action and two postseason rounds, the final 16 teams compete this weekend in four regional finals. Find out who made the cut on the road to Fort Worth.

items.title: Debris from aerial interception strikes Oracle building in Dubai, UAE says
items.summary: The damage to Oracle's building comes after Iran warned it would target U.S. tech companies operating in the Middle East.

items.title: A Billionaire’s Pitch To Cut Power Bills Collides With California’s Real Costs
items.summary: Cleantech investor and governor candidate Tom Steyer says competition with utilities can slash electricity. But the main bill drivers don’t disappear if you change who sells power.

items.title: Are you looking for the best mutual funds for SIPs in April? Here is help for conservative investors
items.summary: ETMutualFunds outlines ready-made SIP portfolios for conservative investors across three investment brackets. The allocation blends large-cap and hybrid funds to balance risk and returns. Selected using in-house criteria like rolling returns and downside risk, these portfolios aim to simplify fund selection for investors seeking stable, long-term wealth creation.

items.title: A Yale economist says AGI won’t automate most jobs—because they’re not worth the trouble
items.summary: Pascual Restrepo's new NBER paper argues it's not about what AI can do. It's about what AI will bother doing—and most human work doesn't make the cut.

items.title: Silver drags commodity ETFs down up to 15% in March. What next for investors?
items.summary: Commodity ETFs declined up to 15% in March, led by sharp losses in silver ETFs amid rising oil prices, geopolitical tensions and tightening liquidity. Gold ETFs also fell as stronger dollar dynamics weighed on prices. The outlook now hinges on inflation trends, liquidity conditions and central bank actions globally.

items.title: Lawyers Are Being Tripped Up By AI Sycophancy When Using AI To Devise Legal Strategies
items.summary: AI sycophancy is tripping up lawyers, especially when devising legal strategies via AI. Here's the rundown, along with what to do about it. An AI Insider scoop.

items.title: College grads in ‘AI-proof’ careers like psychology and education are seeing negative returns on their degrees
items.summary: As AI reshapes white-collar work, new research shows that some of the most popular graduate degrees are actually leaving holders worse off financially.

items.title: How Delta uses Tom Brady to train its 100,000 workforce on leadership and a winner’s mindset
items.summary: Delta CEO Ed Bastian says seven-time Super Bowl champion Tom Brady helps the $42.2 billion airline giant “push the envelope” and continue to succeed at the top.

items.title: Travel guru Rick Steves is happy to pay more taxes
items.summary: The travel icon and millionaire welcomed Washington's new 9.9% income tax on earnings over $1 million.

items.title: U.S. fighter jet shot down in Iran, one crew member rescued, MS NOW reports
items.summary: The U.S. is searching for the second member of the F-15 aircraft's crew, whose whereabouts are unknown, MS NOW reported, citing two U.S. officials.

items.title: Smallcap Stars: 10 stocks rally up to 72% despite 11% Nifty crash in March. Do you own any?
items.summary: Despite an 11% fall in the Nifty during March amid global tensions and rising crude prices, select smallcap stocks delivered strong gains of up to 72%. The list highlights standout performers across sectors that defied the broader market downturn, showcasing resilience and stock-specific momentum in a volatile environment.

items.title: ‘Beetlejuice’ Action Figure Of Winona Ryder’s Lydia Deetz Is In The Works
items.summary: Tim Burton’s “Beetlejuice” star Winona Ryder is getting a detailed action figure of her iconic character Lydia Deetz.
